description_long: This object is a vintage-style control cabinet with a slanted front panel housing multiple analog dials, gauges, and indicator lights. The main body is constructed from dark green painted metal, showing some signs of wear and chipping. The front panel features five large circular gauges with metallic bezels, each labeled with faded white tags, and a vertical row of four red indicator lights on the left side. The sides and back are solid, with the left side displaying three horizontal metallic bars, possibly for reinforcement or ventilation. The underside is open, revealing the hollow interior and mounting brackets. This type of control cabinet is typically used for monitoring and controlling electrical or mechanical systems in industrial or urban utility settings.
description: A dark green, metal control cabinet with multiple analog gauges, indicator lights, and labeled dials on a slanted front panel. The object shows signs of wear and is designed for monitoring or control in industrial or urban environments.
description_view_0: The front view displays the slanted control panel with five analog gauges, four red indicator lights, and several faded labels.
description_view_1: The left side view shows the solid green metal side with three horizontal metallic bars near the bottom.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the open underside and the hollow interior, with no visible controls or features.
description_view_3: The right side view is mostly solid green metal, with the edge of the control panel and some dials visible.
